name: ShapeItem
summary: The Shape class contains information about the shapes in the Tekla Structures shape catalog.

methods.name: CleanAndModify
methods.summary: Cleans and stores the brep into the shape catalog

methods.name: Delete
methods.summary: Deletes a shape from shape catalog based on the shape name.

methods.name: Export
methods.summary: Exports the shape item in *.tsc-format to the to given file name. If path is not given shape is exported to model folder. If filename is empty shape name is used as filename.

methods.name: GetAllMetadataKeys
methods.summary: Get the Shape item's metadata keys.

methods.name: GetInstanceCount
methods.summary: Get the number of instances used in the model of a shape

methods.name: Insert
methods.summary: Inserts a shape to the shape catalog based on the shape geometry (does not allow duplicate geometry).

methods.name: InsertOrGetGuidsOfShapesWithMatchingGeometry
methods.summary: Inserts a shape to the shape catalog based on the shape geometry (does not allow duplicate geometry). If, based on fingerprint, the geometry already exists it populates a list of Guids of shapes using that same geometry.

methods.name: InsertUsingNormals
methods.summary: Inserts a shape to the shape catalog using the shape geometry. Uses vertex normals to determine edge visibility. If the normal vectors of the vertices belonging to one geometrical location are close enough, the edge will be considered smooth and will be marked as hidden.

methods.name: InsertUsingNormalsAllowDuplicates
methods.summary: Inserts a shape to the shape catalog using the shape geometry allowing duplicates. Uses vertex normals to determine edge visibility. If the normal vectors of the vertices belonging to one geometrical location are close enough, the edge will be considered smooth and will be marked as hidden.

methods.name: Modify
methods.summary: Modifies a shape in the shape catalog based on the shape name or if not found, based on shape guid.

methods.name: Rename
methods.summary: Renames a shape in the shape catalog with the given shape name.

methods.name: Select.
methods.summary: Selects the shape from the database based on the name given in this instance.

methods.name: Select(String)
methods.summary: Selects the shape based on the given name from the database.

methods.name: SetHandlePoints
methods.summary: Set the Shape item's Handle Points

methods.name: SetMetadata
methods.summary: Set the Shape item's metadata. Inserts the metadata if key is not found, otherwise modifies existing data.

properties.name: BrepType
properties.summary: Defines BrepType of shapes. @see enum BrepType

properties.name: Extrema
properties.summary: The extrema of the shape as an axis-aligned bounding box, as opposed to object-aligned

properties.name: Fingerprint
properties.summary: A fingerprint value calculated by Tekla Structures to provide quick comparison of geometries to avoid inserting same shape multiple times to the catalog. More rigorous comparison is done only for geometries that result in identical fingerprints.

properties.name: GeometryGuid
properties.summary: A unique identifier of the shape geometry, given initially by BrepStorage. This GUID is used as the body of the filename for the shape geometry information found in the ShapeGeometries directory

properties.name: GeometryHash
properties.summary: This obsolete property can still be used to store and externally provided unique hash value identifying the shape. However, Tekla Structures does not use this value internally for anything. It is strongly recommended to use the newer Fingerprint property for future implementations. That value is automatically calculated by Tekla Structures for all inserted shapes and is used internally for shape identification.

properties.name: Guid
properties.summary: A unique identifier of the actual shape, given initially by the ShapeCatalog. This GUID is used as the body of the filename for the shape information found in the Shape directory

properties.name: HandlePoints
properties.summary: Defines handle points of the shape.

properties.name: IsSolid
properties.summary: Set to true if the shape is detected by TS Core to be a valid solid

properties.name: ShapeFacetedBrep
properties.summary: The data structure containing the geometric information of the shape as a FacetedBRep

properties.name: UpAxis
properties.summary: The direction defining what is understood as "up" in the shape that the API user is providing. Typically this is the z-axis, if the data is in global coordinates, coming from a system where Z-axis points to global up direction. Possible values are: Undefined = 0, X_Axis = 1, Y_Axis = 2, Z_Axis = 3. It is highly recommendable to orient shapes in the Tekla Structures native way, that is x-axis on the extrusion line and Y-axis pointing up. NOTE: Specifying UpAxis to be ShapeUpAxis.Undefined may throw a ‘System.ArgumentOutOfRangeException’ exception in certain operations, because the system can not proceed without that information.
